summary: Verified Electron offline-fallback feature. Static file checks: all required functions/keys/scripts present in main.js, preload.js, package.json, .gitignore, workflow. Frontend Playwright tests: browser-mode hides banner, simulated-Electron-offline shows banner with text 'You're offline', Reconnect + Dismiss buttons work, failed reconnect keeps banner visible, regression confirms no banner on /app, /memory, /download, /tools, /highlights in plain browser. Canvas still renders 6 nodes with banner overlaid. All 5 UI tests passed.

static_file_checks.electron/main.js: PASS — probeOnline, loadOfflineBundle, loadLive, startOnlineProbe all defined. OFFLINE_BUNDLE = path.join(__dirname,'assets','web','index.html') present. did-fail-load handler guards errorCode===-3 and calls loadOfflineBundle() for other codes. Help menu has 'Try to reconnect' and 'Force offline mode'. IPC handlers mm:get-mode and mm:try-reconnect present.
static_file_checks.electron/preload.js: PASS — contextBridge exposes mindMapperDesktop.getMode and mindMapperDesktop.tryReconnect returning ipcRenderer.invoke promises.
static_file_checks.electron/package.json: PASS — 'bundle' script builds frontend + copies build/ into assets/web/. dist, dist:win, dist:mac, dist:linux, dist:all, publish all prefixed with `yarn bundle &&`.
static_file_checks..github/workflows/electron-build.yml: PASS — 'Build the React frontend (static bundle)' and 'Copy React build into electron/assets/web' steps sit between Setup Node and 'Install electron wrapper deps'. Cache key includes frontend/yarn.lock + electron/yarn.lock.
static_file_checks.frontend/App.js HashRouter fallback: PASS — Router = window.location.protocol === 'file:' ? HashRouter : BrowserRouter. OfflineBanner mounted inside Router.

frontend_test_results.test_2_simulated_offline_banner_visible: PASS — banner + [data-testid='offline-reconnect'] + [data-testid='offline-dismiss'] all present, text 'You're offline.' confirmed
frontend_test_results.test_4_failed_reconnect_keeps_banner: PASS — banner persists after tryReconnect returns {ok:false}, button returns from 'Probing…' to 'Reconnect'
frontend_test_results.test_5_regression_no_banner_other_routes: PASS — /app, /memory, /download, /tools, /highlights show no offline banner in plain browser mode

critical_code_review_comments: main.js:207 — 'Try to reconnect' menu item silently no-ops when probe fails; consider a dialog.showMessageBox('Still offline') for better UX.
OfflineBanner.jsx:52-56 — tryReconnect has a dead-code comment block for visual feedback; either implement the failure toast or remove the comments to avoid reader confusion.
main.js:146 — when !HAS_OFFLINE_BUNDLE, the app loads WEB_URL with no retry/fallback; a completely offline first-launch without a bundle will show the raw Chromium error. Acceptable but worth a TODO.

package.json 'bundle' script runs `yarn install --frozen-lockfile` inside frontend every time — CI already has a Build-Frontend step doing the same, so locally this is fine but the double install in CI (workflow step 'Build the React frontend' + electron 'bundle' in `yarn dist`) is redundant if someone runs `yarn dist` in CI. Current workflow avoids this by calling steps separately, but the `yarn dist` path should be documented as local-only.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Electron offline-mode verified via desktop shim injected with page.add_init_script before goto. Static checks cover the bits that can't be browser-tested (file:// HashRouter, main.js IPC handlers, GH workflow bundle steps). Canvas renders 6 nodes with banner overlay — no regression. 401 console errors on /app are pre-existing unauthenticated /api/* calls unrelated to this feature.
rca of the issue: N/A — feature verification only, no bugs found.
